title: His Lachrym�; Or, Mirth Turned To Mourning.
content:
Call me no more,
    As heretofore,
    The music of a feast;
    Since now, alas!
    The mirth that was
    In me is dead or ceas'd.

    Before I went,
    To banishment,
    Into the loathed west,
    I could rehearse
    A lyric verse,
    And speak it with the best.

    But time, ay me!
    Has laid, I see,
    My organ fast asleep,
    And turn'd my voice
    Into the noise
    Of those that sit and weep.
meaning:
"His Lachrym�; Or, Mirth Turned To Mourning." is a quintessential example of Robert Herrick's signature style...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
